FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class Diag

Namespace
FluentGpu.Foundation
Assembly
FluentGpu.Foundation.dll

The standardized, reusable engine-wide diagnostics facility. Every subsystem instruments through it the same way: Diag.Count("text.glyph","rasterized"), Diag.Set("d3d12","glyphInstances", n), Diag.Event("rhi","device-lost"), using (Diag.Time("frame","record")) { … }.

COST MODEL: the recording methods are [Conditional("DEBUG"), Conditional("FLUENTGPU_DIAG")], so on a Release build (with neither symbol defined) the compiler removes the call site AND the argument evaluation entirely — a Diag.Set("text.atlas","nonZero", ExpensiveScan()) costs literally nothing in production. Define FLUENTGPU_DIAG to keep diagnostics in a Release build; toggle at runtime with the AppContext switch "FluentGpu.Diagnostics" or by setting Enabled. Route output by setting Sink.

public static class Diag

CompiledIn 

public const bool CompiledIn = false

Field Value

bool

Enabled

Runtime gate (only consulted when compiled in). Defaults off unless FG_DIAG is set; AppContext switch overrides.

public static bool Enabled

Field Value

bool

Sink

Where Event(string, string)/Dump(string?) output goes (e.g. Console.WriteLine, the devtools panel, a log).

public static Action<string>? Sink

Field Value

Action<string>

Snapshot()

Aggregate snapshot of all values + counters (for the devtools panel / a dump).

public static string Snapshot()

Returns

string

Time(string, string)

Scoped timing: using (Diag.Time("layout","run")) { … }. Internals elide on Release (const-false branch).

public static DiagScope Time(string category, string key)

Parameters

category string
key string

Returns

DiagScope
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
